abstract:
We present Deep Speech, a state-of-the-art speech recognition system built entirely on end-to-end deep learning. Unlike traditional frameworks that rely on complex, hand-engineered processing pipelines and often struggle in real-world conditions, our architecture is significantly streamlined. By replacing labor-intensive components with a direct learning function, the system becomes naturally robust to background noise, reverberation, and speaker variation without the need for a phoneme dictionary or specialized noise-filtering sub-systems. 

Our approach is driven by a highly optimized recurrent neural network (RNN) training engine that leverages multiple GPUs for scalability, alongside novel data synthesis techniques that allow us to generate vast amounts of varied training data efficiently. Deep Speech achieves record-breaking performance on the widely studied Switchboard Hub5’00 benchmark, reaching a 16.0% error rate on the full test set. Furthermore, our system significantly outperforms leading commercial speech engines in challenging, high-noise environments, demonstrating the power of replacing manual engineering with scalable deep learning.
